page_data.title: Listening Time
page_data.learning_objective: Identify and demonstrate good listening behaviour.

activity.instruction: Circle the three pictures that show good listening, then practise the listening pose with a partner.
activity.evidence: Child identifies listening behaviours and demonstrates one.

teacher_prompt.facilitation: Model eyes looking, body still and ears listening without requiring forced eye contact.

parent_prompt.conversation: Take turns telling one short thing while the other person listens and responds.
parent_prompt.home_activity: Take turns telling one short thing while the other person listens and responds.

illustration.scene: Five action cards: facing speaker, quiet body, waiting turn, interrupting, looking at unrelated toy; Star models listening pose.
illustration.focal_point: Five circle choices and one practice prompt.

No more than three speech balloons or long independent writing.

individual_specification.visible_text: Look or face the speaker. Keep a calm body. Wait for your turn. I am listening.

individual_specification.page_specific_prohibition: No demand for eye contact, shame, silent-compliance messaging or ambiguous listening pictures.

compiled_prompt:
# BCube Production Prompt v3 — CC-NURSERY-V3-P024

## 1. Release metadata

- Prompt ID: CC-NURSERY-V3-P024
- Version: 3.1.0
- Book: Communication Champions
- Level: Nursery (3+)
- Module: Module 3 – Friends & Communication
- Page: 24 of 41
- Exact title: Listening Time
- Source: Communication_Champions_Gold_Individually_Rebuilt.xlsx
- Source SHA-256: caecd24a409b4f531b6018b2076d42cc9222af2f198e7c9e5835cde0bc54bfba

## 2. Production command

Create exactly one final, flat, front-facing A4 portrait page for “Listening Time”. Follow this deterministic package only. Produce no explanation, alternate, mockup, or additional page.

## 3. Engine order and precedence

1. Publishing Engine
2. Design Engine
3. Visual Grammar Engine
4. Educational Engine
5. Teaching Engine
6. Parent Partnership Engine
7. Illustration Engine
8. Character Engine
9. Quality Assurance and Prompt Compiler

Precedence: approved page instruction > approved book/level rule > global rule. Do not resolve conflicts by guessing.

## 4. Publishing Engine

- A4 portrait, 210 × 297 mm; 3 mm bleed; minimum 10 mm safe margin; 12 mm binding margin; 300 DPI; CMYK-safe; print-ready.
- Use a 20–28 mm header with the exact title “Listening Time” centred and the official BCube logo reserved at top left.
- Use a 10–14 mm footer with page number 24 only at bottom right.
- Never generate or redraw the logo; apply the official vector asset during final layout.

## 5. Design and Visual Grammar Engines

- Page type: activity_page. Layout anchor: Correct-listening scene plus two comparison boxes.
- Visual/text balance: 70–80% visual and 20–30% text. Use one dominant focal point and a clear title → model → action → response eye path.
- Blank-space rule: Use at most one sentence starter. Maximum two core activities and one optional extension.
- Clean white base, soft BCube pastel accents, thick rounded outlines, large targets, strong contrast, generous spacing, and no visual clutter.

## 6. Educational Engine

- Objective: Identify and demonstrate good listening behaviour.
- Primary activity: Circle the three pictures that show good listening, then practise the listening pose with a partner.
- Secondary activity: Five circle choices and one practice prompt.
- Observable evidence: Child identifies listening behaviours and demonstrates one.
- Developmental response modes: pointing, gesture, one word, short phrase, colouring, tracing, drawing, matching, or adult-supported dictation.

## 7. Teaching Engine

- Facilitation: Model eyes looking, body still and ears listening without requiring forced eye contact.
- Model once, invite participation, pause for processing, scaffold through gesture/choice/model language, and affirm effort without shame.
- Teacher–student interaction must be visible, purposeful, inclusive, and connected to the exact learning objective.
- Assessment evidence: Child identifies listening behaviours and demonstrates one.

## 8. Parent Partnership Engine

- Home connection: Take turns telling one short thing while the other person listens and responds.
- Use common household materials only; do not create a new scored task or require a device, purchase, or printing.

## 9. Illustration and Character Engines

- Composition: Five action cards: facing speaker, quiet body, waiting turn, interrupting, looking at unrelated toy; Star models listening pose.
- Show natural expressions, correct anatomy, inclusive children and families, clear turn-taking, unobstructed activity areas, and editable visible text.
- Star uses the locked bright-yellow rounded five-point identity, expressive face, blue shoes, and small blue cape; Star demonstrates or encourages without completing the child’s answer.

## 10. Locked individual page specification

- Exact child action: Circle the three pictures that show good listening, then practise the listening pose with a partner.
- Exact observable evidence: Child identifies listening behaviours and demonstrates one.
- Exact teacher move: Model eyes looking, body still and ears listening without requiring forced eye contact.
- Exact teacher question: Which picture shows careful listening?
- Exact home connection: Take turns telling one short thing while the other person listens and responds.
- Exact illustration scene: Five action cards: facing speaker, quiet body, waiting turn, interrupting, looking at unrelated toy; Star models listening pose.
- Exact visible text: Look or face the speaker. Keep a calm body. Wait for your turn. I am listening.
- Exact response space: Five circle choices and one practice prompt.
- Page-specific prohibition: No demand for eye contact, shame, silent-compliance messaging or ambiguous listening pictures.
- This section overrides any broader category template or generic instruction elsewhere in the package.

## 11. Negative constraints

- No generated or redrawn BCube logo; reserve the approved position for the official vector asset.
- No unapproved text, labels, tasks, decorative frames, empty balloons, watermarks, QR codes, or third-party marks.
- No unexplained blank areas; every box, line, card, balloon, frame, and open area serves a named learning action.
- No frightening imagery, stereotypes, shame, unsafe behaviour, inaccessible instructions, anatomy errors, cropped key elements, or developmentally excessive writing.
- No mockup, perspective view, page curl, device frame, hands holding the page, or multiple variants.
- No more than three speech balloons or long independent writing.

## 12. Gold QA gate and compiler footer

Verify page identity, geometry, bleed, safe zones, logo reservation, exact title, objective alignment, activity load, teacher interaction, parent extension, expected response, character continuity, inclusion, print suitability, prohibited extras, and unresolved variables. Any critical defect blocks release.
